SurrealDB is a [multi-model Database](https://surrealdb.com/features) that allows you to store and manage data in relational, document, graph, time-series, vector & search and key-value models in one place. 

Unified by a powerful SQL like query language [SurrealQL](https://surrealdb.com/docs/reference/query-language), you can query SurrealDB in ways that are familiar to you, but with the power of a modern database. In addition, SurrealDB is designed to be [distributed and scalable](https://surrealdb.com/docs/architecture), allowing you to build and scale your applications with ease.

Also providing additional query methods like [GraphQL](https://surrealdb.com/docs/learn/querying/graphql/overview), [HTTP](https://surrealdb.com/docs/reference/rest-api/http-protocol) and [RPC](https://surrealdb.com/docs/reference/rest-api/rpc-protocol), you can also query SurrealDB in your native environments using [SDKs](https://surrealdb.com/docs/languages/overview), we support [JavaScript](https://surrealdb.com/docs/languages/javascript), [Python](https://surrealdb.com/docs/languages/python), [Go](https://surrealdb.com/docs/languages/golang), [Rust](https://surrealdb.com/docs/languages/rust), [Java](https://surrealdb.com/docs/languages/java) and [.NET](https://surrealdb.com/docs/languages/dotnet).

Things to remember when working with SurrealDB:

- SurrealDB combines different data models in a single database, allowing you to store and manage data in [relational, document, graph, time-series, vector & search and key-value models in one place](https://surrealdb.com/features)
- It supports multiple query languages including [SurrealQL (native)](https://surrealdb.com/docs/reference/query-language), [RPC](https://surrealdb.com/docs/reference/rest-api/rpc-protocol), [CBOR](https://surrealdb.com/docs/reference/rest-api/cbor-protocol) and [HTTP API](https://surrealdb.com/docs/reference/rest-api/http-protocol)
- Can be [embedded directly in applications](https://surrealdb.com/docs/languages/overview) or [run as a standalone service](https://surrealdb.com/docs/running/overview)
- Provides real-time data synchronization and live queries
- Provides a [REST API](https://surrealdb.com/docs/reference/rest-api/http-protocol) and [WebSocket RPC](https://surrealdb.com/docs/reference/rest-api/rpc-protocol) for real-time updates
- Provides a [GraphQL API](https://surrealdb.com/docs/learn/querying/graphql/overview) for querying data
- Provides a [RPC API](https://surrealdb.com/docs/reference/rest-api/rpc-protocol) for querying data
- Provides a [CBOR API](https://surrealdb.com/docs/reference/rest-api/cbor-protocol) for querying data
- Provides [SurrealQL](https://surrealdb.com/docs/reference/query-language) for querying data


## Get Started

- [SurrealDB Installation Guide](https://surrealdb.com/docs/running/installation): Detailed instructions for installing SurrealDB
- [SurrealDB Running overview](https://surrealdb.com/docs/running/overview): A step-by-step guide to get you started with SurrealDB
- [Get started with SurrealDB Cloud](https://surrealdb.com/docs/build/deployment/surrealdb-cloud/getting-started): A guide to get you started with SurrealDB Cloud 
- [Get started by installing SurrealDB](https://surrealdb.com/docs/running/overview): A guide to get you started in-memory, single-node on-disk, and multi-node scalable deployments of SurrealDB
- [Get started with JavaScript SDK](https://surrealdb.com/docs/languages/javascript/start): A guide to get you started with the JavaScript SDK
- [Get started with Rust SDK](https://surrealdb.com/docs/languages/rust/start): A guide to get you started with the Rust SDK
- [Get started with Java SDK](https://surrealdb.com/docs/languages/java/start): A guide to get you started with the Java SDK
- [Get started with Golang SDK](https://surrealdb.com/docs/languages/golang/start): A guide to get you started with the Golang SDK
- [Get started with .NET SDK](https://surrealdb.com/docs/languages/dotnet/start): A guide to get you started with the .NET SDK
- [Get started with PHP SDK](https://surrealdb.com/docs/languages/php/setup): A guide to get you started with the PHP SDK

## Docs

- [SurrealDB Documentation](https://surrealdb.com/docs): Comprehensive documentation for SurrealDB
- [SurrealQL Documentation](https://surrealdb.com/docs/reference/query-language): Documentation for SurrealDB's query language
- [Surrealist Documentation](https://surrealdb.com/docs/explore/surrealist/getting-started): Documentation for Surrealist, database management interface
- [SurrealDB Cloud Documentation](https://surrealdb.com/docs/build/deployment/surrealdb-cloud/what-is-surrealdb-cloud): Documentation for SurrealDB Cloud, the cloud-based version of SurrealDB
- [JavaScript SDK Documentation](https://surrealdb.com/docs/languages/javascript/start): Documentation for the JavaScript SDK
- [Python SDK Documentation](https://surrealdb.com/docs/languages/python/start): Documentation for the Python SDK
- [Go SDK Documentation](https://surrealdb.com/docs/languages/golang/start): Documentation for the Go SDK
- [Rust SDK Documentation](https://surrealdb.com/docs/languages/rust/start): Documentation for the Rust SDK
- [SurrealDB CLI](https://surrealdb.com/docs/reference/cli): Command line interface documentation
- [HTTP Integration](https://surrealdb.com/docs/reference/rest-api/http-protocol): Guide for using SurrealDB's HTTP API
- [Security Documentation](https://surrealdb.com/docs/learn/security): Security and permissions management

## Raw markdown content

To find the raw markdown content for a route, follow these instructions:

- For a route which matches `/docs/languages/{language}/{...path}`, translate it to either of:
  - `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/surrealdb/docs.surrealdb.com/refs/heads/main/src/content/index/languages/{language}/{path}.mdx`
  - `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/surrealdb/docs.surrealdb.com/refs/heads/main/src/content/index/languages/{language}/{path}/index.mdx`
- For other routes under `/docs/{...path}`, the source usually lives under `src/content/` with the same path segments. Pages from the **index** collection (for example `/docs/architecture`, `/docs/running/overview`) are under `src/content/index/` instead — drop the `/docs` prefix and use `.mdx`:
  - `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/surrealdb/docs.surrealdb.com/refs/heads/main/src/content/index/{path}.mdx`
  - `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/surrealdb/docs.surrealdb.com/refs/heads/main/src/content/index/{path}/index.mdx`
- For collection-backed docs (for example `/docs/reference/query-language/statements/select`, `/docs/learn/querying/graphql/overview`), use the path after `/docs/` under `src/content/`:
  - `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/surrealdb/docs.surrealdb.com/refs/heads/main/src/content/{path}.mdx`
  - `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/surrealdb/docs.surrealdb.com/refs/heads/main/src/content/{path}/index.mdx`

If the ending `/{...path}` is missing, look for `index.mdx` in the matching folder.

### Example

- Website route: `/docs/languages/javascript/api/values`
- Raw markdown URL: `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/surrealdb/docs.surrealdb.com/refs/heads/main/src/content/index/languages/javascript/api/values/index.mdx`
